---
title: "Can we predict rising unmet health need?"

author:
  - name: Felix G.
    affiliation: University of Kansas - Community Data Labs
  - name: Pradeep Kumar
    affiliation: Industry Advisory Board
  - name: William Duncan
    affiliation: University of Kansas, KDSC

date: 2026-04-26
date-modified: 2026-04-28
image: "disabledandhere-036.jpg"
image-alt: "Photo by Chona Kasinger on https://affecttheverb.com/disabledandhere/"

categories: [learning]
# categories - Choose one per post
#   - research
#   - methods
#   - learning
#   - commentary
#   - notes

tags: [public-health, R, tidyverse, data-engineering, data-harmonization, ltertools, survey-data, panel-data, ml, classification, clustering, cross-validation]
# tags - Choose many representative tags
#   - Tools/Software
#     R, Tidyverse, fixest, data.table, caret, ltertools, glmnet
#   - Methods/Techniques
#     regression, panel-data, ML, clustering, classification, inference
#   - Data/Domain
#     education, labor-markets, survey-data, public-health
#   - Workflow/Practice
#     reproducibility, data-engineering, data-cleaning, data-harmonization,
#     pipelines, data-viz

freeze: auto
draft: true
embed-resources: true
---

Adults with disabilities are vulnerable to their health needs being unmet.
